summary: Bulk decompilation pass aimed at matching every remaining 12-byte function. The PR touched 88 files across Dolphin, baselib, game modes, items, fighter, collision, trophy/toy, and debug modules. It added many tiny C implementations, many placeholder declarations for still-unmatched surrounding functions, new headers/prototypes, static/global data declarations needed by those tiny functions, symbol metadata cleanup, and a small tools/easy_funcs.py output improvement. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so rationale beyond the title and diff is limited.
what_changed: Added C source coverage for many tiny functions, often surrounded by `/// #symbol` placeholders for larger still-unmatched functions.
Created or substantially populated previously-empty modules such as `src/melee/db/db_2253.c`, `src/melee/ty/toy.c`, and `src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c`.

Matched simple 12-byte functions as direct global/static field writes or trivial accessors, for example `fn_80226E00` setting `lbl_804D6B50.x = 0`, `un_803102C4` storing an `s8` through `M2C_FIELD`, and `fn_803AC2D4`/`fn_803AC334` updating baselib static state.
Expanded game-mode accessors in `gmmain_lib.c`, including byte getters/setters and pointer-return helpers over `gmMainLib_804D3EE0`, with matching prototypes added to `gmmain_lib.h`.
Added AX callback matches in `AXOut.c`, where `__AXDSPInitCallback` and `__AXDSPDoneCallback` simply set static flags.
Reorganized constants and collision flag defines in `mpcoll.c` near the top of the file to preserve ordering/matching while leaving TODOs about float-order hacks and collision-flag naming.
Updated many headers with address-commented prototypes, frequently using `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, or address-based symbol names where types are still unknown.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` by removing internal GXTexture label symbols and adding/refining data annotations for several game-mode data objects.

Regenerated/rewrote large asm files with absolute-address comments and local `.L_803...` labels, visible in `GXTexture.s` where old synthetic globals like `GXTexture_8033E7D8` became local branch labels.
Changed `tools/easy_funcs.py` so printed function rows show a source `.c` path derived from the unit name instead of the raw unit value.
smart_moves: Targeting all 12-byte functions as a batch is well suited to decomp cleanup because these functions are often trivial stores, setters, getters, flag callbacks, or one-field initializers that can be matched mechanically once data placement is known.

The PR did not force decompilation of large neighboring functions; it left them as `/// #symbol` placeholders while extracting only small known functions, reducing risk and keeping progress incremental.
Using `M2C_FIELD` and padding-based accesses allowed tiny accessors to match without overclaiming full struct layouts.
Adding static/global declarations with address comments gave the matched tiny functions stable storage targets and helped preserve small-data ordering.
Cleaning generated symbol labels out of `GXTexture` reduced pollution from internal jump-table branch labels while keeping true functions like `GXGetTexBufferSize` and `__GetImageTileCount` named.
The `easy_funcs.py` path-display tweak makes future easy-function sweeps more actionable by pointing directly at the expected source `.c` file.
decomp_lessons: For very small functions, prioritize exact data location and type width over semantic naming; a one-instruction store can often be matched with a minimal static/global plus a placeholder type.
It is acceptable to introduce address-based prototypes and `UNK_*` signatures when the function body is not being solved yet, as long as the tiny matched function itself is evidence-backed.
Keep larger functions as `/// #...` gaps while filling small islands of C around them; this lets assembly shrink without requiring full module understanding.
Float and small-data ordering can be decisive for matching; move constants deliberately and document order hacks rather than hiding them.
When branch targets inside an asm function are not real exported symbols, prefer local labels over global symbol-map entries.
Header work is part of matching work: adding prototypes, forward declarations, and minimal structs enables tiny function extraction across modules.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions continue to use address-based `fn_XXXXXXXX` names, such as `fn_80226E00` and `fn_803AC2D4`.
Subsystem-known functions keep subsystem prefixes, such as `gmMainLib_`, `mpColl_`, `hsd_`, `un_`, and item/fighter prefixes.
Headers use address comments before prototypes, for example `/* 3AC2D4 */ UNK_RET fn_803AC2D4(UNK_PARAMS);`.
Unmatched functions are represented in C files with `/// #symbol` placeholders.
Unknown signatures use `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, and `UNK_T` rather than inventing precise types.
The diff itself notes an unresolved naming convention issue in `mpcoll.c`: collision flags named `Collide_*` duplicate concepts like `MPCOLL_RIGHTWALL`, with a TODO to pick one convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Tiny 12-byte functions were matched by expressing one store, flag set, or simple return in C.
Used `M2C_FIELD(base, type, offset)` for accesses into incompletely-understood structs while preserving exact offsets.
Introduced static globals and initialized globals in C to satisfy references from small functions and small-data sections.
Preserved or adjusted float ordering in `mpcoll.c`, explicitly marking `float order hack` TODOs.
Regenerated asm with absolute address comments and `.L_803...` local labels, reducing dependence on artificial global labels.
Removed global symbol entries for GXTexture internal labels in `symbols.txt`, consistent with treating them as local control-flow labels rather than functions.
Adjusted `tools/easy_funcs.py` to report source paths, improving iteration over easy-function candidate lists.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No human rationale beyond the title was captured; inferred lessons are based on changed files and diff evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace `/// #` placeholders added in `db_2253.c`, `toy.c`, or `hsd_3AA7.c`.
Search for later cleanup of `mpcoll.c` collision flag naming TODO.
Check if `gmMainLib_804D3EE0` and related padding structs were later given precise types.
Search for later PRs using the `easy_funcs.py` source-path output for other easy-function sweeps.
Audit remaining `fn_` address-based 12-byte functions after PR 1471.
